data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-01T01:40:27.197Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.content:
Your code **does not use `Thread.Abort`**, `Thread.Interrupt`, `ThreadAbortException`, or `Thread.ResetAbort`. It only uses `Thread.Start()`, `Thread.Join()`, and `Thread.Sleep()` — all of which **work identically in .NET 8** and require no migration.

That said, the code could be modernized using `Task.WhenAll` / `Parallel.ForEach` instead of manual threads, but that's a general modernization choice, not a Thread.Abort migration concern.

**No changes are required for .NET 8 compatibility here.**

The skill description says: "DO NOT USE FOR: code that only uses Thread.Join, Thread.Sleep, or Thread.Start without any abort, interrupt, or ThreadAbortException usage — these APIs work identically in modern .NET and need no migration."


Since their code doesn't actually use any of the problematic threading APIs, I don't need to invoke the migration skill—I should just clarify that their code is already compatible with .NET 8 as-is.
